text.title: Multi-Agent Implementation Checklists
text.summary: Per-pattern design, implementation, and production-readiness checklists for multi-agent systems.

rule.statement: Define clear agent role boundaries and tool access permissions before writing any prompts or code.
rule.rationale: Tool access leakage in frameworks like AutoGen lets agents see tool results intended for others; scoping prevents this.

list.item: Define clear problem scope and agent responsibilities
Choose communication mechanism: typed shared state (Pydantic models), message passing, or tool results
Select framework: LangGraph (state machines), CrewAI (role-based), Google ADK (native primitives), AutoGen (conversations)
Plan error handling and fallback strategies per agent
Design logging and distributed tracing from day one
Establish max_iterations guard before writing the first agent loop
Design: list all task types, group into non-overlapping worker domains
Design: choose routing method — LLM-driven or rule-based; plan fallback for unclassified requests
Implement: supervisor with timeout handling and result aggregation
Implement: workers with isolated tool access and structured output
Test: routing accuracy, each worker in isolation, error fallbacks, concurrent load on supervisor
Production: add circuit breakers for failing workers; set alerting on supervisor failure
Design: define output_key for each stage; verify stage N output matches stage N+1 input schema
Design: plan state storage and cleanup after pipeline completion
Implement: checkpoint persistence between critical stages for recovery
Implement: early termination conditions when validation fails
Test: each stage independently; stage-to-stage data passing; pipeline recovery from checkpoint
Production: add per-stage retries for idempotent stages; measure stage-by-stage latency
Design: map organizational structure; define 2–3 levels maximum
Design: specify task specification format (parent to child) and result format (child to parent)
Implement: timeout cascading across levels
Test: each level in isolation; escalation scenarios; partial failure handling
Production: monitor each level independently; set up distributed tracing
Design: define routing strategy per agent; enforce DAG — no cycles
Design: plan authentication between agents and data sharing boundaries
Implement: message acknowledgment and dead-letter handling
Test: multi-hop routing; network partition scenarios; circular dependency prevention
Production: implement distributed tracing; monitor communication latency; plan agent version compatibility

rule.statement: Block deployment if any of these gates fails: role boundaries undefined, no distributed tracing, no max_iterations guard, no fallback path for primary agent failure.
rule.rationale: Multi-agent failures without traces are nearly impossible to debug; loops without guards drain budgets silently.

list.item: All agent roles clearly defined with scoped tool access
End-to-end latency measured and acceptable
All agents emit logs; distributed tracing works
Agent-to-agent auth implemented; prompt injection mitigations in place
